tasks.name: project chat url
tasks.text_content:
when im in the chat i want to see the projectid and the chat id in the url.
right now it looks like this: https://localhost:5173/p-0c67d3d0cc014c008df123037649b20c/chat
no chat id only the project id. this makes the system confused about which chat you are in and gives errors when reloading the page.
instead, the url should always have the chatid like this https://localhost:5173/p-0c67d3d0cc014c008df123037649b20c/chat/{chatid} and when it encounters a url without the chatid it should automatically pick the top, latest modified chat from that project and use it to create the full url that includes the chat id

tasks.name: configure social media accounts
tasks.text_content:
Follow these instructions. maybe create a task per platform.

# Social Media Platform Setup Guide

This guide provides step-by-step instructions for setting up OAuth credentials for all 13 supported social media platforms in Brand2Boost.

## Table of Contents

1. [LinkedIn](#linkedin)
2. [Facebook](#facebook)
3. [Instagram](#instagram)
4. [TikTok](#tiktok)
5. [Twitter/X](#twitterx)
6. [YouTube](#youtube)
7. [Pinterest](#pinterest)
8. [Reddit](#reddit)
9. [Medium](#medium)
10. [Threads](#threads)
11. [Snapchat](#snapchat)
12. [Bluesky](#bluesky)
13. [Tumblr](#tumblr)

---

## LinkedIn

### Prerequisites
- LinkedIn account
- Access to LinkedIn Developer Portal

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [LinkedIn Developers](https://www.linkedin.com/developers/)
2. Click "Create App"
3. Fill in the app details:
   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Company: (Your company)
   - App logo: (Upload your logo)
4. In the "Auth" tab:
   - Add Redirect URL: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
   - Request "Sign In with LinkedIn" product
5. In "Products" tab, request:
   - "Share on LinkedIn"
   - "Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect"
6. Copy your credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "LinkedInOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `openid`
- `profile`
- `email`
- `w_member_social`

---

## Facebook

### Prerequisites
- Facebook account
- Meta for Developers account

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Meta for Developers](https://developers.facebook.com/)
2. Click "My Apps" → "Create App"
3. Choose app type: "Business"
4. Fill in app details:
   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Contact email: (Your email)
5. In Settings → Basic:
   - Note your App ID and App Secret
6. In Settings → Advanced:
   - Add OAuth Redirect URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
7. In Dashboard, add products:
   - Facebook Login
8. In Facebook Login settings:
   - Valid OAuth Redirect URIs: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
9. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "FacebookOAuth": {
     "AppId": "YOUR_APP_ID",
     "AppSecret": "YOUR_APP_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Permissions
- `public_profile`
- `email`
- `pages_show_list`
- `pages_read_engagement`

---

## Instagram

### Prerequisites
- Instagram Business or Creator account
- Facebook Page connected to Instagram
- Meta for Developers account

### Setup Steps

1. Use the same Facebook app from the Facebook setup above
2. In Meta for Developers dashboard:
   - Add "Instagram Basic Display" product
3. In Instagram Basic Display settings:
   - Add OAuth Redirect URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
4. Create an Instagram Test User (optional for development)
5. Instagram uses the same OAuth credentials as Facebook:
   ```json
   "FacebookOAuth": {
     "AppId": "YOUR_APP_ID",
     "AppSecret": "YOUR_APP_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Permissions
- `instagram_basic`
- `instagram_content_publish`
- `pages_show_list`

---

## TikTok

### Prerequisites
- TikTok Business account
- TikTok for Developers account

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [TikTok for Developers](https://developers.tiktok.com/)
2. Click "Manage Apps" → "Create an App"
3. Fill in app details:
   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Description: (Your description)
4. In "Login Kit" settings:
   - Add Redirect URL: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
5. Request access to required scopes
6. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "TikTokOAuth": {
     "ClientKey": "YOUR_CLIENT_KEY",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `user.info.basic`
- `user.info.profile`
- `video.list`

---

## Twitter/X

### Prerequisites
- Twitter/X account
- Twitter Developer account with Elevated access

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Twitter Developer Portal](https://developer.twitter.com/en/portal/dashboard)
2. Create a new Project and App
3. In App Settings:
   - Set up User authentication settings
   - App permissions: Read
   - Type of App: Web App
   - Callback URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
   - Website URL: `https://localhost:5173`
4. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "TwitterOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `tweet.read`
- `users.read`
- `offline.access`

---

## YouTube

### Prerequisites
- Google account
- Google Cloud Console access

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/)
2. Create a new project or select existing
3. Enable "YouTube Data API v3":
   - APIs & Services → Library
   - Search for "YouTube Data API v3"
   - Click Enable
4. Create OAuth 2.0 credentials:
   - APIs & Services → Credentials
   - Create Credentials → OAuth client ID
   - Application type: Web application
   - Authorized redirect URIs: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
5. YouTube uses Google OAuth credentials:
   ```json
   "GoogleOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/youtube.readonly`
- `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.profile`

### Notes
- YouTube uses the same Google OAuth credentials as Google Ads
- Make sure YouTube Data API v3 is enabled in your Google Cloud project

---

## Pinterest

### Prerequisites
- Pinterest Business account
- Pinterest Developer account

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Pinterest Developers](https://developers.pinterest.com/)
2. Click "My Apps" → "Create App"
3. Fill in app details:
   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Description: (Your description)
4. In App Settings:
   - Add Redirect URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
5. Request access to required scopes
6. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "PinterestOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `user_accounts:read`
- `boards:read`
- `pins:read`

### Notes
- Pinterest API has rate limits (1000 calls per day for free tier)
- Import is limited to 5 boards per user to avoid rate limits

---

## Reddit

### Prerequisites
- Reddit account
- Reddit is Fun API access

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Reddit Apps](https://www.reddit.com/prefs/apps)
2. Scroll down and click "create another app..."
3. Fill in app details:
   - Name: `Brand2Boost`
   - App type: "web app"
   - Description: (Your description)
   - About URL: `https://localhost:5173`
   - Redirect URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
4. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "RedditOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `identity`
- `read`
- `history`

### Notes
- Reddit API requires a User-Agent header (automatically set to "Brand2Boost/1.0")
- Rate limit: 60 requests per minute

---

## Medium

### Prerequisites
- Medium account
- Medium Developer account

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Medium Developer Portal](https://medium.com/me/applications)
2. Click "New Application"
3. Fill in app details:
   - Application name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Description: (Your description)
   - Callback URL: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
4. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "MediumOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `basicProfile`
- `listPublications`

### Notes
- **API Limitation**: Medium API does not provide an endpoint to retrieve user's published articles
- The provider can retrieve user profile and publications list, but not individual articles
- Consider using web scraping or RSS feeds for article content

---

## Threads

### Prerequisites
- Instagram account
- Threads account
- Meta for Developers account

### Setup Steps

1. Use the same Facebook app from the Facebook setup above
2. Threads uses Facebook/Instagram OAuth
3. No additional setup required - uses Facebook credentials:
   ```json
   "FacebookOAuth": {
     "AppId": "YOUR_APP_ID",
     "AppSecret": "YOUR_APP_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Permissions
- `threads_basic`
- `threads_content_publish`
- `threads_read_replies`

### Notes
- Threads uses Facebook OAuth credentials
- Long-lived tokens are valid for 60 days
- Requires Instagram Business or Creator account

---

## Snapchat

### Prerequisites
- Snapchat Business account
- Snapchat Marketing API access

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Snapchat Business Manager](https://business.snapchat.com/)
2. Navigate to Business Details → API Access
3. Create OAuth app:
   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Redirect URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
4. Apply for Marketing API access (requires approval)
5. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "SnapchatOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `snapchat-marketing-api`

### Notes
- **API Limitation**: Snapchat Marketing API is primarily for advertising, not organic content
- Organic content (Stories, Spotlight) is not accessible via API
- The provider can retrieve account information but has limited content import capabilities

---

## Bluesky

### Prerequisites
- Bluesky account
- App password (not your main account password)

### Setup Steps

1. Log in to [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/)
2. Go to Settings → App Passwords
3. Click "Add App Password"
4. Name it: `Brand2Boost`
5. Copy the generated app password (you won't see it again!)
6. **No OAuth configuration needed** in `appsettings.json`

### Authentication Flow

Bluesky uses a different authentication model (AT Protocol) instead of traditional OAuth:

1. User enters credentials in format: `handle:app_password`
   - Example: `myusername.bsky.social:xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx`
2. The provider creates a session using AT Protocol
3. Access tokens are JWT tokens valid for 90 days

### Required Information
- Bluesky handle (e.g., `username.bsky.social`)
- App password (generated in settings)

### Notes
- Does not use traditional OAuth 2.0
- Uses AT Protocol with app passwords
- More secure than using main account password
- Tokens are long-lived (90 days)

---

## Tumblr

### Prerequisites
- Tumblr account
- Tumblr Developer account

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Tumblr Applications](https://www.tumblr.com/oauth/apps)
2. Click "Register Application"
3. Fill in app details:
   - Application name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Application website: `https://localhost:5173`
   - Default callback URL: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
4. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "TumblrOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `basic`
- `write`

### Notes
- Import is limited to 5 blogs per user
- Supports all post types (text, photo, quote, link, chat, audio, video)
- Tracks notes (combined likes and reblogs), tags, and post type

---

## General Configuration

### appsettings.json Template

```json
{
  "LinkedInOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "FacebookOAuth": {
    "AppId": "",
    "AppSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "TikTokOAuth": {
    "ClientKey": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "TwitterOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "GoogleOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "PinterestOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "RedditOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "MediumOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "SnapchatOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  },
  "TumblrOAuth": {
    "ClientId": "",
    "ClientSecret": "",
    "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
  }
}
```

### Shared Credentials

Some platforms share OAuth credentials:

- **YouTube** uses `GoogleOAuth` credentials
- **Instagram** uses `FacebookOAuth` credentials
- **Threads** uses `FacebookOAuth` credentials
- **Bluesky** does not use OAuth (uses app passwords)

### Development vs Production

For production deployments, update the `RedirectUri` in all configurations:

```json
"RedirectUri": "https://yourdomain.com/social-callback"
```

Also update the redirect URIs in each platform's developer console.

### Testing

After configuration, test each platform:

1. Start the backend API
2. Start the frontend
3. Navigate to Connected Accounts
4. Click on a platform button
5. Complete OAuth flow
6. Verify account appears in connected accounts list
7. Click import button to test content import

---

## Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

1. **Invalid Redirect URI**
   - Ensure redirect URI matches exactly in both `appsettings.json` and platform developer console
   - Check for trailing slashes (should not have one)

2. **Missing Scopes**
   - Verify all required scopes are requested in developer console
   - Some platforms require manual approval for certain scopes

3. **Token Expired**
   - Most tokens expire after 60-90 days
   - Use the reconnect button to refresh expired tokens

4. **Rate Limiting**
   - Pinterest: 1000 calls/day
   - Reddit: 60 requests/minute
   - Reduce `MaxItems` in import options if hitting limits

5. **Bluesky Authentication Failed**
   - Verify you're using app password, not account password
   - Format must be: `handle:app_password`
   - Handle should include domain (e.g., `user.bsky.social`)

### Platform-Specific Issues

- **Medium**: Cannot import articles due to API limitations
- **Snapchat**: Limited to marketing data only
- **YouTube**: Ensure YouTube Data API v3 is enabled
- **Threads**: Requires Instagram Business account

---

## Security Best Practices

1. **Never commit credentials to git**
   - `appsettings.json` is in `.gitignore`
   - Use environment variables in production

2. **Use HTTPS in production**
   - Update all redirect URIs to use `https://`
   - Configure SSL certificate

3. **Rotate secrets regularly**
   - Regenerate OAuth secrets every 90 days
   - Revoke old credentials

4. **Limit scope requests**
   - Only request scopes your application needs
   - Remove unused scopes to reduce security risk

5. **Monitor API usage**
   - Track rate limits and quotas
   - Set up alerts for unusual activity

---

## Support

For issues or questions:

1. Check platform-specific developer documentation
2. Review error messages in browser console and API logs
3. Verify credentials are correctly configured
4. Test OAuth flow in platform's API playground (if available)

# Social Media Platform Setup Guide

This guide provides step-by-step instructions for setting up OAuth credentials for all 13 supported social media platforms in Brand2Boost.

## Table of Contents

1. [LinkedIn](#linkedin)
2. [Facebook](#facebook)
3. [Instagram](#instagram)
4. [TikTok](#tiktok)
5. [Twitter/X](#twitterx)
6. [YouTube](#youtube)
7. [Pinterest](#pinterest)
8. [Reddit](#reddit)
9. [Medium](#medium)
10. [Threads](#threads)
11. [Snapchat](#snapchat)
12. [Bluesky](#bluesky)
13. [Tumblr](#tumblr)

---

## LinkedIn

### Prerequisites
- LinkedIn account
- Access to LinkedIn Developer Portal

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [LinkedIn Developers](https://www.linkedin.com/developers/)
2. Click "Create App"
3. Fill in the app details:
   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Company: (Your company)
   - App logo: (Upload your logo)
4. In the "Auth" tab:
   - Add Redirect URL: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
   - Request "Sign In with LinkedIn" product
5. In "Products" tab, request:
   - "Share on LinkedIn"
   - "Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect"
6. Copy your credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "LinkedInOAuth": {
     "ClientId": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `openid`
- `profile`
- `email`
- `w_member_social`

---

## Facebook

### Prerequisites
- Facebook account
- Meta for Developers account

### Setup Steps

1. Go to [Meta for Developers](https://developers.facebook.com/)
2. Click "My Apps" → "Create App"
3. Choose app type: "Business"
4. Fill in app details:
   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Contact email: (Your email)
5. In Settings → Basic:
   - Note your App ID and App Secret
6. In Settings → Advanced:
   - Add OAuth Redirect URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
7. In Dashboard, add products:
   - Facebook Login
8. In Facebook Login settings:
   - Valid OAuth Redirect URIs: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
9. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "FacebookOAuth": {
     "AppId": "YOUR_APP_ID",
     "AppSecret": "YOUR_APP_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Permissions
- `public_profile`
- `email`
- `pages_show_list`
- `pages_read_engagement`

---

## Instagram

### Prerequisites
- Instagram Business or Creator account
- Facebook Page connected to Instagram
- Meta for Developers account

### Setup Steps

1. Use the same Facebook app from the Facebook setup above
2. In Meta for Developers dashboard:
   - Add "Instagram Basic Display" product
3. In Instagram Basic Display settings:
   - Add OAuth Redirect URI: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
4. Create an Instagram Test User (optional for development)
5. Instagram uses the same OAuth credentials as Facebook:
   ```json
   "FacebookOAuth": {
     "AppId": "YOUR_APP_ID",
     "AppSecret": "YOUR_APP_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Permissions
- `instagram_basic`
- `instagram_content_publish`
- `pages_show_list`

---

### Prerequisites
- TikTok Business account
- TikTok for Developers account

   - App name: `Brand2Boost`
   - Description: (Your description)
4. In "Login Kit" settings:
   - Add Redirect URL: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
5. Request access to required scopes
6. Copy credentials to `appsettings.json`:
   ```json
   "TikTokOAuth": {
     "ClientKey": "YOUR_CLIENT_KEY",
     "ClientSecret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET",
     "RedirectUri": "https://localhost:5173/social-callback"
   }
   ```

### Required Scopes
- `user.info.basic`
- `user.info.profile`

# Social Media Import - Setup Instructions for ClickUp Task

## Prerequisites
- Access to LinkedIn Developer Portal (https://www.linkedin.com/developers/)
- LinkedIn app already created (Client ID: `770k2mszhs3pl9`)
- Local development environment running (Frontend on HTTPS port 5173)

---

## Step 1: Configure LinkedIn Developer Portal

### 1.1 Access Your LinkedIn App
1. Go to https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps
2. Sign in with LinkedIn credentials that have access to the app

### Issue 2: OAuth popup blocked
**Cause**: Browser is blocking popups

**Solution**:
1. Enable popups for `localhost:5173`
2. In Chrome: Click the popup-blocked icon in address bar → "Always allow"
3. Try connecting again

### Issue 3: "Token exchange failed"
**Cause**: Backend can't reach LinkedIn API, or credentials are wrong

**Solution**:
1. Check backend console logs for detailed error

     -d "client_secret=WPL_AP1.RQ7rEHzqCRQSpFjB.ZdJbCA=="
   ```
4. Check firewall/proxy settings

### Issue 4: Import returns 0 posts
**Cause**: LinkedIn API limitations or permissions

**Known LinkedIn API Limitations**:
- Reading your **own** posts requires special permissions
- LinkedIn restricts access to older content
- Some accounts may have limited API access
- App must be verified for production use

**Solution**:
1. Check LinkedIn Developer Portal → Products → Ensure correct products are enabled
2. Request "Marketing Developer Platform" access if available
3. For testing, try a different LinkedIn account

**Cause**: Projects folder doesn't exist or no write permissions

**Solution**:

   mkdir -p "c:\stores\brand2boost"
   ```
2. Check folder permissions (write access required)
3. Verify `ProjectSettings:ProjectsFolder` in `appsettings.json`

### Issue 6: HTTPS certificate errors
**Cause**: Browser doesn't trust self-signed certificate

**Solution**:
1. In Chrome, click "Advanced" → "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)"
2. Or regenerate certificates using `mkcert`:
   ```bash
   # Install mkcert
   choco install mkcert  # Windows

   # Generate trusted certificates
   cd C:\Projects\client-manager\ClientManagerFrontend
   mkcert localhost
   ```
3. Restart frontend dev server

     }
   }

   - Hazina SQLite stores support automatic token encryption

5. **Monitor Rate Limits**:
   - LinkedIn has rate limits (varies by product)
   - Implement exponential backoff retry logic
   - Consider caching API responses

tasks.text_content:
Task: Brand Header / Footer as Reusable Brand Assets
Goal
Introduce a generic, reusable Brand Document Fragment component that can be instantiated as multiple analysis fields (e.g. header/footer variants) and automatically used when generating official documents such as:
Business plans
Restaurant menus
Product catalogs
Flyers
Company letters
These fragments must be brand-aware, partially editable, regenerable, and reusable across document types.
Core Concept
1. Brand Document Fragment (New Component)
Create one generic component type (recommended name up to Claude):
Example: BrandDocumentFragment
Used for both headers and footers
Configured via analysis fields (not hardcoded roles)
This component:
Is a brand asset
Is composable (layout + slots)
Is generated by an agent
Produces structured output, not static images
Analysis Field Usage Model
Multiple analysis fields can reference this same component, for example:
company_letter_header
company_letter_footer
menu_header
menu_footer
product_catalog_header
product_catalog_footer
Each analysis field:
Uses the same underlying component
Has its own configuration (role, layout hints, slot schema)
Has one active instance (for now, no versioning)
Component Structure (Recommended)
Separation of Concerns (Required)
The component must separate:
Layout definition
Structure
Ordering
Logo placement
Dividers / spacing
Content slots
Editable fields
Brand-derived fields
Static vs non-generatable fields
Composable via:
Prompt instructions
Analysis-field configuration
Data Sources & Inputs
Always Included (if present)
Brand profile
Narrative
Color scheme
Typography
Logo(s)
Included via Relevancy Search (Embeddings)
Other chats
Gathered business data
Analysis fields
Imported documents
Non-Generatable Fields
Some fields must never be invented, e.g.:
Phone number
Address
Bank details
VAT number
These must come from:
Existing gathered data in the document store
And be injected into the fragment where applicable
Editable Fields Model
Each fragment defines its own schema, per analysis field
Example slots:
company_name
address
phone
email
bank
slogan (optional)
User edits are allowed
On regeneration:
Generated parts update
User-provided factual data is preserved
Shared data (e.g. phone/address) should automatically propagate when changed in the document store.
Output Format (Chosen)
Canonical Output: Structured JSON
The generated fragment must output:
A structured JSON layout model
Including:
Visual hierarchy
Text blocks
Image references (logo)
Styling tokens (colors, font roles)
This JSON must be convertible to:
Office document fragments (DOCX)
PDF
PNG/SVG (optional, later)
This keeps the system:
Flexible
Regenerable
Future-proof
Agent Responsibilities
Single Dedicated Agent (Required)
Create or reuse one brand synthesis agent responsible for:
Interpreting brand assets
Designing header/footer layout
Filling content slots
Explaining design decisions
The agent:
May use common sense
May use image tools if needed
Must follow brand constraints
Must not invent factual data
Explainability
Each generated fragment must include:
Feedback / rationale section
Explaining layout & styling decisions
 (similar to other generated items)
Regeneration Rules
Regeneration is suggested or manual, not silent
Automatic regeneration may be triggered by chat events
Actual regeneration only happens if:
All required prerequisites exist (logo, colors, etc.)
Changes that should trigger regeneration suggestions:
Logo update
Color scheme update
Typography update
Narrative update
UI / Visibility
Headers and footers must be:
Viewable as standalone artifacts
Openable from the analysis-field menu
Preferably fullscreen preview
Integration with Document Generation
Document generators (e.g. business plan):
Reference the relevant analysis fields
Inject header/footer fragments during generation
Example:
Business plan uses company_letter_header and company_letter_footer
Configuration
New component type must be registered in:

c:\stores\brand2boost
Analysis fields remain the control layer
Claude Code should determine:
Naming
Config schema
Minimal changes needed to support this cleanly
Versioning & Editing (Scope Control)
Versioning: out of scope for now
Editing:
Basic editable fields required
WYSIWYG is optional:
Claude Code may implement minimal structured editing
Or defer full WYSIWYG to later iterations
Definition of Done (Phase 1)
A header and footer can be generated as brand fragments
They are stored as structured JSON
They are visible as analysis items
The business plan generator uses them
Regeneration works with preserved factual data
Visual perfection is not required in this phase.
Explicit Non-Goals (Phase 1)
Full visual editor
Advanced theming
Version history
User-facing configuration of component logic
Final Note to Claude Code
This task introduces a new class of reusable, brand-aware document fragments.
 Design for extensibility, not perfection.
 Prefer clear data models + agent logic over rigid templates.

tasks.name: Opening message when starting a new chat in an existing project
tasks.text_content:
Functional Description
Opening Question & New Chat Initialization Logic (Brand2Boost)
This document defines two distinct mechanisms for initializing conversations:
New Project creation from the homepage/projects list
New Chat creation inside an existing project
These mechanisms are intentionally separate and must not share logic beyond generic chat rendering.
1. Homepage Rotating Question (New Project Entry Point)
Purpose
To provide a lightweight, engaging entry point that lets a user start a new project by answering a single opening question.
Source of Questions
Questions are stored in:

stores/brand2boost/opening-questions.json
File format:

["First opening question with **highlighted parts**","Another opening question with **styled emphasis**"]
This file:
Contains a JSON array
Is the only source of homepage opening questions
Must be editable without code changes
If this is not currently the case, it must be refactored so it is.
Selection & Display Rules
On homepage/projects list load:
Select one question at random from opening-questions.json
This selection happens once per page load
The selected question must remain stable until reload
Display requirements:
Render with an “AI typing” animation (if already supported)
Preserve existing rich formatting:
**bold** segments rendered with the current gradient styling
The selected question must be stored in client state as:

selectedHomepageQuestion
Project Creation Flow
When the user types a response and presses Enter:
The client sends a Create Project request containing:
The exact selectedHomepageQuestion (including markup)
The user’s response text
The backend:
Creates a new project
Creates an initial chat
Stores two messages, in order:
Assistant: selectedHomepageQuestion
User: user response
The client:
Optimistically renders the project and chat immediately
Navigates to the new chat view
Reconciles with backend data once returned (backend is source of truth)
Critical Consistency Rule (Bug Fix)
When the chat screen loads after project creation:
Do not select or generate a new opening question
The first assistant message shown must be the same question originally shown on the homepage
The chat UI must always render from chat history returned by the backend after reconciliation.
2. New Chat Inside an Existing Project (Second Entry Point)
Purpose
To start a fresh conversation within the context of an existing project, guided by AI understanding of that project.
This mechanism is not related to the homepage rotating questions.
Trigger
User presses the “+” (New Chat) button inside an existing project.
Flow Overview
Client immediately creates a new chat (optimistic UI).
As soon as the chat is created:
The client requests an AI-generated starter question.
The generated question becomes:
The first assistant message in the new chat
Persisted in backend chat history
The question is streamed/rendered as it is generated.
AI Context Construction Rules
The AI-generated starter question must be based on relevance, not static prompts.
Always Include (if present)
Core project metadata:
Brand name
Brand profile / description
Other rudimentary project fields
Include via Relevancy Search
Using embeddings-based relevance:
Previous chats in the project
Gathered data objects
Analysis fields
Imported documents / files
Any other stored project artifacts
The client does not manually assemble all content.
 It requests a relevance-based context set from the backend/LLM layer.
Formatting Rules
The AI-generated starter question:
Does not require gradient styling
May use simple formatting:
Rendered as bold text if supported
No requirement to match homepage visual style.
Error Handling
If an error occurs while generating the AI starter question:
The chat should display a generic assistant message:

An error has occurred while communicating with the server.
No technical details are exposed in the UI.
This behavior must be documented as an architectural decision in Markdown documentation for future middleware/error-layer enhancements.
3. State & Source-of-Truth Rules
The client:
Optimistically renders projects and chats
Must always reconcile and update state from backend responses
The backend:
Is the final source of truth for:
Chat history
Opening questions actually used
Message ordering
4. Explicit Non-Goals
This task does not include:
Personalization strategies beyond relevance search
Time-based reflections or scenario templates
Middleware error classification
Changes to onboarding flows other than consistency fixes
Styling redesign
Summary (for Claude Code)
Two opening-question systems exist and must remain separate
Homepage rotating questions are:
Static, JSON-based, visually rich, user-answered
New-chat questions inside projects are:
AI-generated, context-aware, relevance-driven
The homepage-selected question must persist unchanged into the created chat
Backend always wins; client is optimistic but reconciling
This description is intentionally implementation-agnostic and should be used as the behavioral contract when designing the technical solution.

tasks.name: add policy and terms documents to frontend
tasks.text_content:
Facebook requires us to have the following documents:
privacy policy
terms & conditions
deletion conditions
Other social media may also require such documents/pages or other ones.

/privacy-policy → should display the privacy policy
/terms-and-conditions → should display terms and conditions
/deletion-conditions → should display deletion conditions

etc. (whatever else is required by facebook, linkedin etc)


1. Mandatory Legal & Compliance Pages (Required by Facebook, LinkedIn, Google, Apple)
/privacy-policy
Required by: Facebook, LinkedIn, Google, Apple, Microsoft, TikTok
Must include:
What personal data is collected
Purpose of collection
Third-party sharing (incl. Meta, LinkedIn APIs)
Data retention period
User rights (GDPR/CCPA)
Contact details for data controller
/terms-and-conditions
Required by: Facebook, LinkedIn, Apple
Must include:
User responsibilities
Service limitations
Liability disclaimer
Governing law
Account termination conditions
/data-deletion (Facebook naming preferred)
Alias:
/deletion-conditions
/account-deletion
Required by: Facebook (explicit), Google
Must include:
How users can request data deletion
What data is deleted
Timeframe
Contact or automated flow
Link from privacy policy
Facebook explicitly checks for a public deletion instructions page, even if deletion is in-app.
2. Strongly Recommended (Often Implicitly Required)
/cookie-policy
Required by: EU law (GDPR/ePrivacy)
Expected by: Facebook, Google
Must include:
Cookie categories
Consent mechanism
Tracking pixels (Meta, LinkedIn Insight Tag)
/data-processing
Useful for: Enterprise clients, LinkedIn
Covers:
Legal basis for processing
Sub-processors
International transfers
/security
Expected by: LinkedIn, GitHub, enterprise OAuth reviews
Covers:
Data protection measures
Encryption
Access control
Incident response
3. OAuth / API Review–Specific Pages
/app-description
Required by: Facebook, LinkedIn, Google (App Review)
Purpose:
Explain what your app does
Why social login is needed
What scopes are used
/permissions
Required by: Facebook App Review
Must explain:
Each requested permission
Why it is needed
How data is used
/support
Required by: Apple, Google
Must include:
Contact email or form
User support instructions
4. User Rights & Transparency (GDPR / CCPA)
/user-rights
Recommended
Right to access
Right to correction
Right to erasure
Right to portability
/consent-preferences
Recommended
Manage marketing consent
Manage data sharing
Opt-out options
5. Trust & Platform Credibility (Optional but Valuable)
/about
Used by reviewers to validate legitimacy
/contact
Often required for escalation and legal notices
/status (optional)
Operational transparency (popular with dev-focused platforms)
6. Minimal “Safe” Page Set (Recommended Baseline)
If you want a single clean setup that passes all reviews, implement at least:

/privacy-policy
/terms-and-conditions
/data-deletion
/cookie-policy
/app-description
/permissions
/support
/contact
Notes Specific to Facebook & LinkedIn
Facebook explicitly checks:
Privacy Policy URL
Data Deletion Instructions URL
Clear explanation of permissions
LinkedIn focuses on:
Legitimate business purpose
Data minimization
Professional use case clarity

User rights (GDPR/CCPA)
Contact details for data controller
/terms-and-conditions
Required by: Facebook, LinkedIn, Apple
Must include:
User responsibilities
Service limitations
Liability disclaimer
Governing law
Account termination conditions
/data-deletion (Facebook naming preferred)
Alias:
/deletion-conditions
/account-deletion
Required by: Facebook (explicit), Google
Must include:
How users can request data deletion
What data is deleted
Timeframe
Contact or automated flow
Link from privacy policy
Facebook explicitly checks for a public deletion instructions page, even if deletion is in-app.
2. Strongly Recommended (Often Implicitly Required)
/cookie-policy
Required by: EU law (GDPR/ePrivacy)
Expected by: Facebook, Google
Must include:
Cookie categories
Consent mechanism
Tracking pixels (Meta, LinkedIn Insight Tag)
/data-processing
Useful for: Enterprise clients, LinkedIn
Covers:
Legal basis for processing
Sub-processors
International transfers
/security
Expected by: LinkedIn, GitHub, enterprise OAuth reviews
Covers:
Data protection measures
Encryption
Access control
Incident response
3. OAuth / API Review–Specific Pages
/app-description
Required by: Facebook, LinkedIn, Google (App Review)
Purpose:
Explain what your app does
Why social login is needed
What scopes are used
/permissions
Required by: Facebook App Review
Must explain:
Each requested permission
Why it is needed
How data is used
/support
Required by: Apple, Google
Must include:
Contact email or form
User support instructions
4. User Rights & Transparency (GDPR / CCPA)
/user-rights
Recommended
Right to access
Right to correction
Right to erasure
Right to portability
/consent-preferences
Recommended
Manage marketing consent
Manage data sharing
Opt-out options
5. Trust & Platform Credibility (Optional but Valuable)
/about
Used by reviewers to validate legitimacy
/contact
Often required for escalation and legal notices
/status (optional)
Operational transparency (popular with dev-focused platforms)
6. Minimal “Safe” Page Set (Recommended Baseline)
If you want a single clean setup that passes all reviews, implement at least:

/privacy-policy
/terms-and-conditions
/data-deletion
/cookie-policy
/app-description
/permissions
/support
/contact
Notes Specific to Facebook & LinkedIn
Facebook explicitly checks:
Privacy Policy URL
Data Deletion Instructions URL
Clear explanation of permissions
LinkedIn focuses on:
Legitimate business purpose
Data minimization
Professional use case clarity

add MPesa as payment method (paystack)
tasks.text_content:
MPesa is not in Stripe by default but it can be integrated using paystack.

No, Stripe does not directly support M-Pesa, but businesses can integrate them together through a service like Paystack, which is owned by Stripe and provides access to local African payment methods like M-Pesa. This allows businesses to use Stripe for global payments and Paystack to handle local M-Pesa transactions for customers in Kenya. 
Stripe for global payments: Businesses can use Stripe to accept payments from customers around the world.
Paystack for local payments: To accept M-Pesa, businesses must set up a separate Paystack account, which integrates with Stripe and provides access to M-Pesa and other local payment methods.
Combined integration: By using both Stripe and Paystack, businesses can offer a comprehensive payment solution that caters to both international and local customers.
